description: Add gradient-boosted classifiers to evaluate CLI — GitHub trovlunde/stock_analyzer#6

userStories.title: Add lightgbm dependency and register LightGBM classifier
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want LightGBM available as a sklearn-compatible classifier in the movement-classification registry so boosters can join GridSearchCV + TimeSeriesSplit.
acceptanceCriteria: `uv add lightgbm`; import succeeds in CI without network
`LGBMClassifier` registered in `classifiers` (helpers) or a dedicated booster registry consumed by `compare_classifiers`
`get_classifier_param_grid()` includes a reasonable LightGBM param grid (e.g. n_estimators, max_depth, learning_rate)
LightGBM entry follows existing `set_params` / `fit` / `predict` sklearn-compatible pattern
uv run pytest passes

userStories.title: Extend evaluation layer for optional classifier selection
userStories.description: As a quant hobbyist, I want `compare_classifiers` and `evaluate_all_classifiers` to accept an optional classifier filter so boosters run in the same pipeline without changing holdout split sizes.
acceptanceCriteria: `compare_classifiers(..., classifier_names=None)` runs only named classifiers when provided; default runs existing sklearn grid
`evaluate_all_classifiers(..., classifier_names=None)` passes filter through; `holdout_size` and `temporal_holdout_split` semantics unchanged
Return dict shape unchanged: `comparison_results`, `best_classifier`, `holdout_metrics`, `holdout_size`
uv run pytest passes

userStories.title: Add --classifier flag to compare CLI subcommand
userStories.description: As a user, I want `stock-analysis-evaluate compare --classifier lightgbm` so I can evaluate boosters with the same `--holdout-months` and `--extra-features` flags.
acceptanceCriteria: `compare` subcommand accepts `--classifier` (e.g. `lightgbm`, `all`, or sklearn name); documented in argparse help
Flag wired through `cmd_compare` → `evaluate_all_classifiers`
`--holdout-months`, `--extra-features`, `--threshold`, and `--test-size` behavior unchanged
uv run pytest passes

userStories.title: P1 pytest: LightGBM in compare output with matching holdout size
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a fast regression test on synthetic prepared data proving LightGBM participates in comparison and holdout row count matches the sklearn baseline.
acceptanceCriteria: New test uses synthetic prepared-data fixture (no live yfinance)
Fixture has enough rows and class variety for temporal split (train ≥30, holdout ≥10)
With `--classifier lightgbm` or `classifier_names=['LightGBM']`, `comparison_results` includes LightGBM
Same fixture: `holdout_size` with LightGBM equals `holdout_size` with sklearn-only default run
uv run pytest passes

userStories.title: Document booster vs ensemble tradeoffs in README and CLI help
userStories.description: As a user, I want guidance on when to prefer LightGBM boosters over sklearn ensembles (speed, tabular performance, install weight).
acceptanceCriteria: README ML Model Evaluation section mentions `--classifier lightgbm` and booster vs ensemble tradeoffs
CLI `--help` on `compare` references classifier selection and when to prefer boosters
uv run pytest passes
